items.description: Heavy retained-heat chamber for baking and slow roasts. These are anchored in communal kitchens or militia logistics bays — salvage on-site rather than improvised in occupied stacks.
Bench press for sizing brass and seating bullets. Production gear lives in guarded armory wings — not corridor craft fodder.
Vised receiver work surface with gauges and punches. Militia benches stay bolted down; hauling one up from surplus is loot logic, not a quick build.
Scrap grate, pierced drum skirt, and a skimmed accelerant sump. Enough to simmer tins on the lower tiers when the mains are dead — world heat hook TBD.
Tin reservoir, scav wax melt, twisted cotton wick. Stale-air stairs and blackout landings — low light without burning batteries.
Bent sheet trough with pop-riveted end caps screwed along a leaky riser cheek or bulkhead seam—bleeds condensation past bedding instead of tarp circus acts. Routed drips hook TBD.
Fire-facing clay brick slabs lashed with scrap banding and scav cloth so they hold warmth against a blackout sleep roll—the low-tech alternative to duvets.
Classic spring-loaded snap jaws on a battered wooden base baited from custodial stock—narrow corridor choke, snap audio, infestation hook TBD.
